summary: Russian forces launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile strike and 90 drones across multiple regions, causing civilian injuries and damage to fuel infrastructure. Drone activity was reported near several towns in Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, including attacks on an oil depot and a cinema. Ukrainian authorities reported casualties in Zaporizhzhia beach attack and injuries in Sumy and Kharkiv regions.
key_points: Russian forces attacked Ukrainian oil depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts with drones, halting operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
An Iskander-M ballistic missile strike and 90 drones hit Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, injuring civilians and damaging infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Four people, including three children, were injured in a Russian strike on a Zaporizhzhia beach (Source: @UkraineNow/82150).
A drone attack on a cinema in Konotop, Sumy oblast, wounded multiple civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
UAVs were observed flying near Pidryanka in Kharkiv oblast heading south (Source: @kpszsu/66286).
UAVs were reported near Prudyanka in Kharkiv oblast moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/66286).
UAV activity was noted near Komishuvakha heading toward Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66313).
UAVs were seen over the northern outskirts of Sumy moving west or southwest (Source: @kpszsu/66271).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces launched a coordinated strike using an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones across Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv, causing civilian injuries and damaging fuel infrastructure. Drone activity was reported near multiple towns in Sumy, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, including attacks on an oil depot and a cinema. Ukrainian authorities recorded casualties from a beach attack in Zaporizhzhia and injuries in Sumy and Kharkiv.

points: Ukrainian officials reported that drone attacks halted operations at oil depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Four civilians, three of them children, were injured during a Russian strike on a beach in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82150).
A cinema in Konotop, Sumy oblast, was hit by drones, resulting in at least one leg injury and a neck wound (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
In Kharkiv oblast, a drone strike damaged a building façade, an electrical line, a garage and three cars, injuring one woman (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian authorities reported that two workers at a Zaporizhzhia fuel station were hospitalized with minor injuries after a drone strike (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian forces employed an Iskander-M ballistic missile in a night attack on Sumy, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ninety drones were launched in the same operation, targeting civilian and infrastructure sites across multiple oblasts (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑eastern direction, indicating potential air strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Additional reports indicated continued tactical aviation activity on the north‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/66297).
Russian UAVs were observed flying over Sumy oblast heading westward from the northern outskirts of Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/66271).
UAVs were sighted near Prudyanka in Kharkiv oblast moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/66286).
UAVs were observed near Pidryanka in Kharkiv oblast heading south (Source: @kpszsu/66286).
UAVs were reported near Komishuvakha heading toward Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66313).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned for the north‑eastern direction, affecting eastern front zones (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
UAVs were seen over the northern outskirts of Sumy moving west or southwest (Source: @kpszsu/66271).
UAV activity was reported in the north‑eastern direction, suggesting possible air operations near the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Additional alerts indicated continued tactical aviation activity on the north‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/66297).
UAVs were observed near the northern outskirts of Sumy moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/66271).
No specific ground engagements were detailed for the northern front in the provided messages.
Drone attacks on oil depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts disrupted fuel supply (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
A beach strike in Zaporizhzhia injured four civilians, including three children (Source: @UkraineNow/82150).
Iskander-M missile strike included southern regions such as Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
No further specific southern front ground actions were detailed in the messages.
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑eastern direction, raising the threat of air strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
A repeat alert indicated ongoing tactical aviation activity on the north‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/66297).
Ninety drones were launched in a coordinated night operation across multiple regions (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
UAV sightings were frequent in Sumy, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, indicating high drone traffic (Sources: @kpszsu/66286, @kpszsu/66313, @kpszsu/66271).
Ukrainian air defenses faced a surge of strike UAVs, temporarily overwhelming defenses in Sevastopol according to separate reports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).

key_developments: Russian forces used an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones in a coordinated night strike on Sumy, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Drone attacks halted operations at oil depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, disrupting fuel logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
A Russian strike on a Zaporizhzhia beach injured four civilians, including three children (Source: @UkraineNow/82150).
UAVs were observed near Prudyanka and Pidryanka in Kharkiv oblast, moving southward, indicating active drone corridors (Source: @kpszsu/66286).
UAVs were sighted near Komishuvakha heading toward Zaporizhzhia, suggesting targeting of logistics routes (Source: @kpszsu/66313).
Tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑eastern axis, raising the risk of air strikes near front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Ukrainian authorities reported civilian injuries from a drone attack on a cinema in Konotop, Sumy oblast (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone operations demonstrate a shift toward high‑intensity, multi‑domain strikes targeting both civilian infrastructure and logistical nodes. The concentration of UAV activity in Sumy, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia oblasts strains Ukrainian air defenses, especially after the reported overload in Sevastopol. Continued tactical aviation presence on the north‑eastern front increases the risk of air assaults on rear areas, while attacks on fuel depots threaten supply lines critical to Ukrainian operations.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have mobilized emergency services to treat injured civilians and are likely reinforcing air defense assets around high‑risk corridors. Reports of halted fuel depot operations suggest a need for alternative logistics routes and increased protection of energy infrastructure. The documented civilian casualties may prompt heightened public alertness and potential adjustments in civil defense protocols in the affected regions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths should focus on the identified corridors near Prudyanka, Pidryanka, Komishuvakha and the northern outskirts of Sumy to anticipate future strikes. The surge in drone usage indicates a possible stockpile depletion by Russian forces, which could affect the tempo of future attacks. Coordination with allied air defense systems may be required to mitigate the overload risk observed in Sevastopol and other contested airspaces.
